sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Atlético Madrid have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches with 3 wins and 2 losses, demonstrating resilience in attack with 28 goals scored this season. Valencia, on the other hand, are unbeaten in their last 5 matches but have drawn 4, indicating a struggle to convert chances into wins with only 14 goals scored in the league.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head encounters, Atlético Madrid have dominated Valencia, winning both matches with an aggregate score of 6-0. Their defensive solidity is evident as they kept clean sheets in both games, while Valencia failed to score.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Atlético Madrid rank 4th in La Liga with 31 points, boasting strong attacking stats (28 goals, 4th in the league) and a solid defense (15 conceded, 2nd best). Valencia, sitting 16th with 15 points, have struggled offensively (14 goals) and defensively (23 conceded), which could be a concern against Atlético's high-pressing game.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: Julián Álvarez (Atlético Madrid) has been pivotal with 7 goals this season, overperforming his xG, while Antoine Griezmann's historical impact against Valencia (2 goals, 2 assists in recent H2H) makes him a threat. For Valencia, Hugo Duro's 5 goals are crucial, but their lack of consistent scoring threats may limit their chances.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Atlético Madrid's superior form, historical dominance over Valencia, and Sofascore's prediction of a 78% chance of a home win, they are clear favorites. Valencia's recent draws suggest they might keep it tight, but Atlético's attacking prowess and home advantage at Riyadh Air Metropolitano should secure the victory.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Atlético Madrid leads Valencia by a narrow 1-0 scoreline at the Riyadh Air Metropolitano. The home side took the lead early in the 17th minute through a well-taken goal by Koke. Despite the scoreline, Valencia has shown resilience and created several dangerous opportunities. Both teams have had their moments, but Atlético Madrid will be pleased to hold the advantage going into the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attacking momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The game started with Valencia showing strong attacking intent, dominating the early minutes with sustained pressure. However, momentum shifted around the midpoint of the first half as Atlético Madrid found their rhythm, leading to a crucial spell that resulted in their goal. After conceding, Valencia struggled to regain their earlier dominance but managed to push back with several attacking waves towards the end of the half. Atlético Madrid responded with a late flurry of activity, trying to extend their lead before the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key team statistics
halftimeSections.text: Atlético Madrid has been clinical with their chances, converting one of their big opportunities into a goal. Their defensive solidity is evident with a high percentage of duels and aerial challenges won. Valencia, on the other hand, has controlled more of the ball and entered the final third more frequently. Despite creating more big chances, their inability to finish has cost them dearly, with missed opportunities proving to be the difference so far.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Player performances
halftimeSections.text: For Atlético Madrid, Koke has been the standout performer, not only scoring the opener but also contributing defensively. Alexander Sørloth has been a constant threat up front, getting into dangerous positions. On Valencia’s side, Hugo Duro has been lively, though wasteful in front of goal with a couple of big misses. Pepelu has tried to dictate play from midfield, creating chances for his teammates.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: The second half promises to be an intriguing battle as Valencia will likely push for an equalizer, given their attacking intent and higher expected goals in the first half. Atlético Madrid will aim to capitalize on counter-attacks while maintaining their defensive structure. Discipline will be key for the home side, as they’ve already picked up a couple of yellow cards. Valencia must improve their finishing if they are to turn their dominance in possession into something tangible.
